questionHtml:
What would be the electrode potential, E⦵, of the Mn2+ (aq)|Mn (s) half-cell if Fe3+ (aq)|Fe2+ (aq) is used as the reference standard?

Mn2+ (aq) + 2e− ⇌ Mn (s)           E⦵ = −1.18 V

Fe3+ (aq) + e− ⇌ Fe2+ (aq)          E⦵ = +0.77 V

A.  −1.95 V

B.  −0.41 V

C.  +0.41 V

D.  +1.95 V
